briefing.opening: Good morning. On April 2, 2026, as a climate change expert spokesperson and science journalist, I will begin today's climate briefing.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis: Comprehensive Warnings Domestically and Internationally
sections.content: Today, severe impacts and warnings due to climate change have been reported comprehensively, both domestically and internationally. Domestically, agriculture and natural ecosystems are facing direct threats, including instability in apple production [3, 18], the spread of forest pests and diseases [10, 17], and concentrated management of drought-vulnerable areas [11, 13, 15, 16]. The expansion of the habitat of the Japanese mistletoe, a climate change indicator plant, clearly demonstrates the speed of these changes [2, 9]. Internationally, severe threats of climate change across society have been reported, such as the 'fast-food ocean' phenomenon due to marine nutrient depletion [139], research findings linking extreme heat to low birth weight in newborns [141], frequent occurrences of large-scale wildfires [142], and the 'shrink-swell' phenomenon affecting homes in France [146]. Notably, new research indicating that Arctic sea ice loss is causing simultaneous heatwaves in Europe and East Asia [153] warns that the climate crisis is triggering a global chain reaction beyond specific regions.

sections.title: 🌱 Diverse Efforts by Domestic Society to Achieve Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Diverse efforts are underway across the nation to achieve carbon neutrality, which is central to addressing the climate crisis. Various local governments such as Yeongcheon City, Bucheon City, Guro-gu, Goesan-gun, and Buyeo-gun, along with businesses and civic organizations, are contributing to expanding carbon sinks through Arbor Day tree-planting events [14, 24, 26, 27, 41, 44, 45, 48, 51, 52, 53, 56, 57, 64, 68, 71, 72, 74, 77, 80, 81]. KEPCO KDN's 'Eco-Road' operation [19, 20, 21, 47, 49, 55, 73, 76] and Bucheon City's utilization of waste wood [22, 30, 39, 79] are cited as excellent examples of practicing carbon neutrality in daily life. Furthermore, programs that encourage citizen participation and raise awareness, such as Namdong-gu's customized education for youth [4, 32], Hwaseong City Environmental Foundation's 'Climate Action Opportunity Income' [62], and Cheongju City's 'Carbon Neutrality Point System' enrollment event [63], are also actively being promoted.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Policy Challenges
sections.content: President Lee announced that, learning from the energy crisis, the government will accelerate the energy transition towards renewable energy and expand loans and subsidies to 1.1 trillion won [84, 85, 93, 94, 98, 101, 122, 124, 125]. The Vice Minister of Climate also emphasized the need to expand renewable energy to simultaneously achieve energy security and carbon neutrality, reporting a supplementary budget proposal that increased related funds by 282.8 billion won [23, 120, 126, 134]. Various local governments, including Gunsan City, Yeongju City, Chuncheon City, and Jeongeup City, are actively promoting support for new and renewable energy installations and integrated support projects. Notably, Chuncheon City declared itself an energy self-sufficient city centered on floating solar power [90, 91, 92, 95, 102, 105, 106, 107, 109, 110, 111, 112, 114, 115, 116, 123, 133]. However, alongside the warning that 'without renewable energy, there will be no exports' [89], policy challenges and the need for social consensus are also simultaneously highlighted, including difficulties in fulfilling RE100 due to insufficient renewable energy [130], arguments for the exclusion of nuclear power [83], and debates surrounding the renewable energy transition amidst the 'war supplementary budget' [131].

sections.title: 🌍 International Cooperation and Capacity Building for Climate Change Response
sections.content: International cooperation and capacity-building efforts to address climate change are also gaining attention. The signing of a business agreement between the Korea Climate Change Institute and Chuncheon National University of Education to foster outstanding talent [1] is a good example of developing domestic climate leadership, and the collaboration between the Seoul Metropolitan Area Meteorological Administration and Gyeonggi Province to jointly respond to extreme weather [8] demonstrates the importance of inter-agency cooperation for climate change adaptation. Internationally, a climate action residency program in Hawaii is encouraging artists' participation to explore creative climate solutions [143], and practical support measures are being established, such as awarding over $25 million to support farmers combating the impacts of climate change [149]. This indicates that the climate crisis requires a holistic response across social, economic, and cultural sectors, beyond being merely an environmental issue [137, 138, 151].

briefing.closing: As you have seen in today's briefing, the climate crisis is impacting all aspects of our lives, and numerous challenges remain to be addressed alongside various domestic and international response efforts. We must continue to foster a more sustainable future through sustained attention and cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.
